name: Mixer Stereo
description: This algorithm is a stereo mixer – it mixes mono or stereo inputs to a stereo output. Up to four aux send busses are available, which can be switched to pre- or post-fade. Sends may also be set to be mono or stereo.
gui_description: The display shows a level meter for each channel. The thin line at the top indicates 0dBFS; if a channel goes over this, a clip indicator will show (a small rectangle over the 0dBFS line). The chevrons to the right of the level meters indicate the mixer gains. Mute and Solo are indicated with a small ‘M’ and ‘S’ respectively. Muted level meters are grayed out.

specifications.name: Sends
specifications.description: The number of aux sends per channel.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

parameters.name: Destination
parameters.description: The output bus for the send.

parameters.name: Pre/post
parameters.description: Whether the send is Pre-fade or Post-fade.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: Whether the send is mono or stereo. Stereo sends use the destination bus and the next one up.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

parameters.name: Input right
parameters.description: The channel right input bus (if stereo).

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: A text name for the channel for identification.

input_ports.name: Mono/Stereo Inputs
input_ports.description: Mixes mono or stereo inputs to a stereo output.

output_ports.name: Stereo Output
output_ports.description: The final stereo output of the mixer.
